home.platformVersion: Platform v2.0 — Cloud LLM + RAG Pipeline
home.heroSub: AI platform for compliance assessment of ISO 27001:2022 and TCVN 11930:2017. Integrated with Multi-LLM Fallback and Semantic RAG Search.
home.footer: CyberAI Assessment Platform · FastAPI · Next.js · Open Claude · © 2026
home.featureChatDesc: AI assistant for ISO 27001 and TCVN 11930 queries with streaming response
home.featureAssessmentDesc: Multi-standard assessment form with RAG Auditor and Multi-LLM Fallback
home.featureTemplatesDesc: Real-world network system templates for assessment testing and experimentation
home.featureAnalyticsDesc: Performance monitoring, assessment history and real-time service status

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Application preferences
settings.languageNote: Language preference is stored locally. Switch between English and Vietnamese.
settings.guideAssessmentDesc: Detailed guide on using the Assessment feature with 4-step process, AI modes, and report analysis.
settings.promptsChatDesc: Customize system prompts for the chatbot (default, RAG, web search, log analysis). Isolated from the assessment module.
settings.promptsAssessmentDesc: Customize Phase 1 (chunk) and Phase 2 (report) prompt templates plus the evidence instruction block. Fully isolated from Chat AI.

assessmentMode.title: Assessment Mode
assessmentMode.description: Choose how AI models run during ISO assessment. Applies to new assessments started from this browser.

cloud.label: Cloud
cloud.desc: Use cloud LLMs for both phases — fastest, requires API key.

local.label: Local
local.desc: Run everything on local models — fully offline, slower.

hybrid.label: Hybrid (Recommended)
hybrid.desc: Local model for analysis, cloud model for report formatting — balanced speed and privacy.

prompts.chatDesc: Edit the system prompts used by the chatbot. Each prompt keeps a default value and can be reset at any time.
prompts.assessmentDesc: Edit the prompt templates used by the assessment pipeline. Keep required placeholders intact so the pipeline keeps working.
prompts.pickOne: Pick a prompt on the left to start editing.
prompts.resetConfirm: Reset this prompt to its default value?
prompts.unsavedConfirm: You have unsaved changes. Continue and discard them?

docs.architectureDesc: Overall architecture of the CyberAI platform including FastAPI backend, Next.js frontend, LocalAI, and ChromaDB.
docs.apiDesc: Complete REST API documentation for all endpoints including chat, assessment, standards, and system routes.
docs.deploymentDesc: Production deployment guide with Docker Compose, nginx configuration, SSL setup, and scaling strategy.
docs.chatbotRagDesc: How the AI chatbot works with Retrieval-Augmented Generation, prompt engineering, and model fallback chains.
docs.chromadbDesc: Vector store patterns, data persistence, backup strategy, and semantic search configuration.
docs.algorithmsDesc: Weighted scoring algorithm, compliance calculation, category breakdown, and risk assessment methodology.
docs.analyticsDesc: Prometheus metrics, health checks, log aggregation, system stats, and alerting configuration.
docs.benchmarkDesc: How to benchmark AI model quality across Local, Hybrid, and Cloud modes with structured test cases.
docs.caseStudiesDesc: Real-world assessment examples from banks, hospitals, startups, and government agencies.
docs.isoFormDesc: Guide to the 4-step assessment wizard, control checklists, evidence upload, and report generation.
docs.backupDesc: Data backup procedures, ChromaDB persistence, assessment data protection, and disaster recovery.
docs.markdownRagDesc: Standards for markdown document formatting optimized for RAG chunking and semantic search.
docs.multiStandardDesc: Architecture for supporting multiple compliance standards (ISO, NIST, PCI DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, SOC 2).

chatbot.startConversationSub: Ask any question about ISO 27001, information security, or compliance.
chatbot.enterToSend: Enter to send · Shift+Enter for newline
chatbot.noResponseFromModel: No response received from model. Please try again.
chatbot.streamInterrupted: Streaming was interrupted while the tab was inactive. Please resend if the answer is incomplete.
chatbot.timeoutError: Request timeout (30 min). Local model is loading — please try again later.
chatbot.networkError: Network error: Cannot connect to server. Check your network connection and retry.
chatbot.suggestedPrompt3: How are ISO 27001 and SOC 2 different?

stream.fallback: ⚠️ Stream failed, switching to standard mode...

assessment.standardHelp: The selected standard determines the Controls Checklist in Step 3.
assessment.scopeHelp: Define which systems / departments are included in this assessment.
assessment.scopeSysPlaceholder: e.g.: ERP SAP, Customer Portal, Core Banking
assessment.scopeDeptHelp: List department names, separated by commas. AI will focus on assessing these departments.
assessment.scopeSysHelp: Briefly describe the system name and function. AI will focus risk analysis on that system.
assessment.vpnLabel: System has VPN configured for remote employees
assessment.controlsHelp: Check (✓) the controls that your organization has actually implemented:
assessment.notesPlaceholder: Any information about weaknesses or risks you are concerned about...
assessment.errorModelLoad: LocalAI cannot load model (insufficient RAM or model file not downloaded).
assessment.errorModelLoadFix: Switch to Hybrid or Cloud mode in Step 4 → re-assess.
assessment.errorBusy: AI is busy processing another task.
assessment.errorBusyFix: Wait 1-2 minutes and try again, or switch to Hybrid/Cloud mode.
assessment.processingTabAway: You can switch to another tab — the system processes in the background and auto-updates when done. Go to History tab to see results.
assessment.exportNote: Structured data: compliance %, risk counts, weight breakdown, top gaps. Use for external dashboard/BI integration.
assessment.historyNote: RAG processes in background. Reports saved on server · Total: {count} assessments
assessment.historyEmptyHint: Complete your first assessment from the Form Input tab.
assessment.historyDeleteConfirm:
Delete assessment for "{org}" ({date})?
This action cannot be undone.
assessment.templatesDesc: Network architecture data, access management and compliance levels of real organizations.
assessment.templatesGuideDesc: Sample data from real organizations (banks, hospitals, startups, government agencies...) pre-configured with infrastructure and compliance controls.
assessment.templatesGuideUsage: How to use: Select a template → System auto-fills form → You can make edits → Click "Assess" for AI analysis.
assessment.templatesGuideFor: Suitable for: Demo, training, benchmark, comparing compliance levels across organization types.
assessment.scopeFullTip1: Ideal for first-time assessment or preparing for ISO certification
assessment.scopeFullTip2: Includes all departments, systems, and processes in the organization
assessment.scopeFullTip3: AI will perform a comprehensive assessment and provide company-wide priority recommendations
assessment.scopeDeptTip1: Ideal for internal assessment of individual departments
assessment.scopeDeptTip2: e.g.: Only assess IT Department + Accounting Department
assessment.scopeDeptTip3: List department names separated by commas
assessment.scopeSysTip1: Ideal for assessing a specific application / system
assessment.scopeSysTip2: e.g.: Core Banking, ERP SAP, Customer Portal
assessment.scopeSysTip3: AI will focus risk analysis on that system
assessment.step4Desc: Describe your network architecture or system specifics so AI can provide the most accurate assessment:
assessment.topologyGuideNote: Image/file reading is not yet supported. Please describe in text following these suggestions:
assessment.topologyNetwork1: Number of VLANs (Virtual LAN) and purpose: Server, User, Guest, Management
assessment.topologyNetwork2: DMZ Zone: present or not, contains Web Server, Mail Gateway, DNS
assessment.topologyNetwork3: Firewall: model, position at network perimeter / between security zones
assessment.topologyNetwork4: Internet uplink: number of ISPs, BGP failover / load balancing
assessment.topologyServer1: Physical servers vs VM (Virtual Machine) — platform: VMware / Hyper-V / KVM
assessment.topologyServer3: Services: AD (Active Directory), DNS, DHCP, Web, Database, Mail
assessment.topologyServer4: HA (High Availability) / Clustering / Load Balancer if available
assessment.topologySecurity1: EDR/XDR (Endpoint Detection & Response): product name, deployment scope
assessment.topologySecurity2: SIEM (Security Information & Event Management): solution, log retention period
assessment.topologySecurity3: VPN (Virtual Private Network): IPSec / SSL, who can use it
assessment.topologySecurity5: WAF (Web Application Firewall), IDS/IPS, DLP (Data Loss Prevention) if available
assessment.topologyExample: Network split into 5 VLANs: Server (10), User (20), Guest (99), Management (1), DMZ Zone (50). FortiGate 200F Firewall at perimeter, block all inbound by default. DMZ contains Nginx Web Server + Mail Gateway. Core Banking on 2 Dell R750 HA Cluster servers, connected via Cisco Catalyst 9300. SSL VPN for 200 remote employees. Wazuh SIEM collecting logs from all servers, 12-month retention.
assessment.networkTopologyPlaceholder:
Example:
- Network split into 3 VLANs: Server (10), User (20), Guest WiFi (99)
- All traffic goes through FortiGate Firewall.
- Internal servers not exposed to the internet.
- SSH port closed, access only via internal VPN.
assessment.notesGuide5: Expected security budget for this year
assessment.aiModeFlowTitle: 🔐 Data Processing Flow by AI Mode
assessment.aiModeLocalDesc: Data never leaves the server. Phase 1 + Phase 2 both processed locally. Suitable for: air-gap, classified data, military. Drawback: slower, basic reports.
assessment.aiModeHybridDesc: Infrastructure data only processed locally. Phase 1 security analysis on local → GAP results (no IP, config) sent to cloud for professional report formatting. Balance of security & quality.
assessment.aiModeCloudDesc: ⚠️ All data sent to cloud. Including: infrastructure info, firewall, server, SIEM. Most detailed reports, fastest speed. Only use when data is not sensitive.
assessment.implementedHint: This control has been marked as implemented. Ensure evidence documentation is updated and stored properly.
assessment.notImplementedHint: Control not yet implemented. Refer to assessment criteria and required evidence list to plan implementation.
assessment.noControlDetail: No detailed information available for this control. You can attach implementation evidence below.
assessment.evidenceHelpText: Attach evidence files (PDF, images, config, log...) for {controlId}. Supports drag & drop of multiple files. File content will be analyzed by AI when submitting assessment.

formIso.evidenceRequiredHint: At least one evidence file is required.

analytics.pageSubtitle: System monitoring, assessment history and standards management
analytics.noAssessmentsHint: Run an assessment from the Assessment page to see results here.
analytics.deleteWarning: This action permanently deletes the assessment report and cannot be undone.
analytics.noCustomStandardsHint: Upload a JSON/YAML file above to add one.
analytics.benchmarkDesc: Compare analysis quality between: SecurityLM (LocalAI) vs Meta-Llama (LocalAI) vs Cloud.
analytics.benchmarkCompare: Compare Local vs Cloud (run twice per test case)
analytics.benchmarkVsAudit: This Tool vs Official IT Audit
analytics.benchmarkConclusion: Conclusion: This tool does not replace a formal IT Audit — it is a support tool that helps any IT staff (without deep security expertise) perform a preliminary self-assessment, identify weaknesses, and prepare for a formal audit.
analytics.failedNote: This report failed in an older session. Use "Reuse Form" to re-run on the current system.
analytics.benchmarkToolPoint2: AI auto-generates GAP analysis and Risk Register
analytics.benchmarkToolPoint4: LocalAI: data never leaves the server (air-gap)
analytics.benchmarkToolPoint5: Best for: pre-audit self-assessment, training, internal benchmarking
analytics.benchmarkAuditPoint2: Staff interviews and real evidence verification
analytics.benchmarkAuditPoint3: Takes 2–6 weeks for a medium-sized organization
analytics.benchmarkAuditPoint4: Results are legally binding certification reports
analytics.benchmarkAuditPoint5: Best for: ISO certification, legal compliance, client reporting

standards.pageSubtitle: Upload custom standards (JSON/YAML) → Backend parses + ChromaDB indexes → Assessment form auto-renders.
standards.noCustomHint: Upload a JSON/YAML file above to add a new standard.

templates.pageSubtitle: Infrastructure profiles based on real Vietnamese enterprises with verified ISO 27001 / TCVN 11930 certifications. Each template includes cited sources.
templates.noTemplates: No templates available for this standard.
templates.uploadEvidenceHint: Drop files here or click to upload
templates.previewUnavailable: Preview not available for this file type.

evidenceLibrary.title: Sample Evidence Library
evidenceLibrary.subtitle: Realistic sample evidence documents mapped to ISO 27001:2022 Annex A controls. Click the eye icon to preview content, or download to use as templates for your own evidence.
evidenceLibrary.noEvidence: No evidence documents in this category.
evidenceLibrary.mandatoryTitle: Evidence is mandatory for compliance assessment
evidenceLibrary.mandatoryDesc: An organization cannot be assessed as compliant without providing evidence of implementation. Each control requires documented proof (policies, procedures, logs, screenshots, configurations) that demonstrates the control is actively implemented and maintained.

sampleEvidence.infoSecPolicyDesc: Comprehensive ISMS policy covering classification, access control, incident management, and business continuity. Maps to A.5.1–A.5.4.
sampleEvidence.accessControlMatrixDesc: Role-based access matrix for ERP, cloud, and network systems with PAM and lifecycle management. Maps to A.5.15–A.5.18.
sampleEvidence.incidentResponsePlanDesc: Full IRP with classification, response phases, evidence collection, and testing schedule. Maps to A.5.24–A.5.28.
sampleEvidence.securityTrainingDesc: Training program with completion records, phishing simulation results, and background screening procedures. Maps to A.6.1, A.6.3, A.6.8.
sampleEvidence.physicalSecurityDesc: Physical access controls, CCTV surveillance, environmental controls, and clean desk audit results. Maps to A.7.1–A.7.14.
sampleEvidence.techBaselineDesc: Endpoint security, network segmentation, cryptography standards, SDLC security gates, and backup procedures. Maps to A.8.1–A.8.34.
sampleEvidence.loremPolicyDesc: Generic placeholder PDF for testing upload flow.
sampleEvidence.screenshot1Desc: Placeholder screenshot for testing image preview.
sampleEvidence.screenshot2Desc: Placeholder screenshot for testing image preview.

guide.pageSubtitle: Assessment (Information Security Assessment) · AI Auditor · ISO 27001 · TCVN 11930
guide.readyToStartDesc: Go to the Assessment page to evaluate your system now.
guide.sec1Desc: The Assessment (Information Security Assessment) feature allows you to evaluate compliance with international and Vietnamese security standards automatically using AI.
guide.sec1Bullet1: Identify GAPs (gaps) between current security posture and standard requirements
guide.sec1Bullet2: Generate a Risk Register with priority levels
guide.sec1Bullet3: Create professional Markdown reports exportable to PDF
guide.sec1Bullet4: Support multiple standards: ISO 27001, TCVN 11930, NIST CSF, PCI DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, SOC 2
guide.sec1TcvnSuitable: Government agencies, IT systems under Decree 85
guide.sec2Desc: The Assessment uses a 4-step sequential wizard. Each step collects different information so the AI has enough data for the most accurate analysis.
guide.sec2Step1Desc: Company information, standard selection, assessment scope
guide.sec2Step2Desc: Servers, firewall, VPN, backup, SIEM, cloud
guide.sec2Step4Desc: Describe topology, choose AI mode, review → Start Assessment
guide.sec2TipText: The form automatically saves a Draft to the browser. You can close the page and come back — your data will still be there. The ✕ Clear button will delete the draft.
guide.sec3Desc: The first step collects basic information about the organization and selects the assessment standard.
guide.sec3FieldStandardDesc: Select the security framework for assessment. Determines the Checklist in Step 3.
guide.sec3FieldEmployeesDesc: Total number of people in the organization
guide.sec3FieldItSecDesc: Number of people in IT & Security department
guide.sec3ScopeFullWhen: First-time assessment, preparing for ISO certification
guide.sec3ScopeFullEx: Assess entire company with 5 departments, 3 systems
guide.sec3ScopeDeptEx: Assess only IT Department + Accounting Department
guide.sec4Desc: Describe existing technology infrastructure. The more detailed, the more accurate the AI assessment.
guide.sec4FieldFirewallEx: FortiGate 200F at perimeter, Palo Alto PA-220 internal
guide.sec4FieldIncidentsDesc: Number of security incidents in the past year
guide.sec4FieldVpnDesc: Is VPN configured for remote employees
guide.sec4TipText: Not all fields are required. However, Firewall, AV/EDR, Backup are very important — they help AI accurately assess compliance for controls related to A.8 Technology.
guide.sec5Desc: This is the most important step. You check (✓) the Controls that your organization has actually implemented in practice.
guide.sec5GroupOrgContent: Policies, roles, IAM, suppliers, incidents, BCP/DR, legal
guide.sec5GroupPeopleContent: Hiring, training, NDA, remote work, incident reporting
guide.sec5GroupPhysicalContent: Physical security, CCTV, UPS, maintenance, device disposal
guide.sec5GroupTechContent: Endpoint, PAM, MFA, malware, backup, SIEM, firewall, crypto, SDLC
guide.sec5WeightDesc: Each control has 1 of 4 weight levels. Compliance score is calculated using weighted scoring — important controls have greater impact on overall compliance %.
guide.sec5HighExample: A.5.9 Asset Inventory, A.8.22 Network Segmentation
guide.sec5MediumExample: A.5.7 Threat Intelligence, A.8.6 Capacity Planning
guide.sec5LowExample: A.5.6 Expert Group Liaison, A.8.17 NTP
guide.sec5ExampleStep1: Open the A.5 Organizational group by clicking the title
guide.sec5ExampleStep2: Check ✓ A.5.1 — Information Security Policy (if already published)
guide.sec5ExampleStep3: Click the ⓘ icon next to a control to view requirements + upload evidence
guide.sec5ExampleStep4: Use the Select all in this group button if an entire group is fully implemented
guide.sec5ExampleStep5: Watch the real-time % compliance bar at the top
guide.sec5WarningText: Only check controls that are actually implemented. Incorrect checks will skew the AI assessment. If implementation is in progress, do not check — instead, note it in the Notes section in Step 4.
guide.sec6Desc: The final step before submitting the assessment. You describe the network architecture (topology) in text and select the AI mode.
guide.sec6TopologyDesc: This is a free-text section — write in natural language. The AI will analyze it to understand the system architecture.
guide.sec6TopologyExample: 📝 Example of a complete topology description:
guide.sec6NotesBullet1: Recent incidents: Phishing (email fraud), Ransomware, DDoS (denial of service attacks)
guide.sec6SummaryDesc: Before clicking Start Assessment, the system displays a summary table:
guide.sec7Desc: The system supports 3 AI modes with different security and quality levels. Choose the appropriate mode based on the sensitivity of your data.
guide.sec7LocalConDesc: More basic reports, requires strong GPU/RAM
guide.sec7HybridDataDesc: Processed locally only — IP, configs not sent to cloud
guide.sec7HybridFitDesc: Most organizations — balance of security & quality
guide.sec7CloudDataDesc: Everything sent to cloud (including infrastructure, firewall, server info)
guide.sec7SecurityWarningText: If your organization handles sensitive data (customer information, healthcare data, financial data), choose Local Only or Hybrid. Cloud mode sends all infrastructure information to the internet.
guide.sec8Desc: After the AI completes analysis, the Result tab displays a report with multiple sections:
guide.sec8GaugeDesc: Displays overall weighted compliance %. Color changes by level: green ≥80%, blue ≥50%, yellow ≥25%, red <25%
guide.sec8BadgeDesc: ✅ High Compliance | 🟡 Partial Compliance | 🟠 Low Compliance | 🔴 Non-compliant
guide.sec8ModelChipsDesc: AI models used (e.g.: SecurityLM, Semantic RAG)
guide.sec8CategoryDesc: Progress bar table for each controls group, displaying:
guide.sec8CategoryBullet1: Category name (e.g.: A.5 Organizational, A.8 Technological)
guide.sec8CategoryBullet3: Controls passed / total controls in group
guide.sec8DashRiskDesc: Count GAPs by severity: Critical / High / Medium / Low
guide.sec8DashWeightDesc: Separate compliance % for each weight level (critical, high, medium, low)
guide.sec8DashGapsDesc: Top 8 unimplemented controls with critical/high severity
guide.sec8Level1Desc: Ready for certification audit, only need to finalize details
guide.sec8Level2Desc: Good foundation, need to add more controls
guide.sec8Level3Desc: Just starting, need to build a prioritized implementation roadmap
guide.sec8Level4Desc: Very high risk, immediate action required
guide.sec9HistoryDesc: All assessments are saved on the server. The History tab displays:
guide.sec9HistoryAction3: Retry : Return to Step 4 to resubmit (when failed)
guide.sec9TemplatesDesc: The template library contains real-world system data (banks, hospitals, startups...) pre-configured. Useful for: demo, training, benchmark, comparison.
guide.sec9TemplateStep3: View info: employees, servers, IT staff, compliance level
guide.sec9TemplateStep5: System auto-fills the entire form → edit if needed → Assess
guide.sec10Desc: Each control can have evidence attached — files proving actual implementation. The AI will analyze file content when generating reports.
guide.sec10HowStep1: In Step 3, click the ⓘ icon next to any control
guide.sec10HowStep2: The detail panel opens — scroll to the Upload Evidence section
guide.sec10HowStep3: Drag & drop files into the upload area, or click 📎 Choose file
guide.sec10HowStep4: Supports multiple files at once, up to 10MB/file
guide.sec10Ev1Desc: Approved policy PDF file, signed by management
guide.sec10Ev2Desc: Training slides, attendee list, test scores
guide.sec10Ev3Desc: EDR dashboard screenshot, virus scan report, policy config
guide.sec10Ev4Desc: SIEM config export, sample log, dashboard screenshot
guide.sec10Ev5Desc: Firewall rule export (.conf), network diagram, access policy
guide.sec10TipText: You can click 👁️ to preview uploaded file content. Click ✕ to delete a file. Total attached files are shown in the badge next to the title.
guide.sec11Desc: After the assessment is complete, you have multiple ways to export & share reports:
guide.sec11CopyReportDesc: Copy entire Markdown content to clipboard
guide.sec11CopyReportWhen: Paste into email, Slack, Confluence, Notion
guide.sec11ViewPdfDesc: Open new tab with formatted HTML → Ctrl+P to print/save as PDF
guide.sec11DownloadPdfDesc: Server generates PDF and downloads directly
guide.sec11DownloadJsonDesc: Export structured data (compliance %, risk counts, weight breakdown)
guide.sec11DownloadJsonWhen: Integrate with external dashboard/BI (Grafana, Power BI)
guide.sec11NewAssessmentDesc: Return to input form for new assessment
guide.sec11NewAssessmentWhen: Assess different organization or after improvements
guide.sec11ReassessDesc: Resubmit form with a different AI mode
guide.sec12Desc: Compliance score is not a simple count (e.g.: 62/93 = 66.7%). The system uses weighted scoring — critical controls have 4× the impact of low controls.
guide.sec12Formula: % Compliance = (Total points of checked controls) / (Maximum total points) × 100
guide.sec12FormulaNote: Where: critical = 4 points, high = 3, medium = 2, low = 1
guide.sec12Explanation: → Because Organization A checked many critical controls (18/22), the weighted score is higher.
guide.sec13Q1: How long does an assessment take?
guide.sec13Q1Note: You don't need to wait — you can switch tabs or close the browser. The system processes in the background and saves results. Go back to the History tab to check when it's done.
guide.sec13Q2: What does 'could not load model' or 'rpc error' mean?
guide.sec13Q2Cause: Cause: LocalAI cannot load the model — usually due to insufficient RAM/VRAM or model file not downloaded.
guide.sec13Q2Fix: Solution: Switch to Hybrid or Cloud mode in Step 4 and reassess. Or check if the localai Docker container is running.
guide.sec13Q3: What's the difference between 'simple count' and 'weighted score'?
guide.sec13Q3Count: Simple count: 62/93 controls = 66.7% — each control is equal.
guide.sec13Q3Weighted: Weighted score: Critical controls (×4) have more impact than low (×1). So if you check many critical controls, the % will be higher than 66.7%.
guide.sec13Q3Note: The system always uses weighted scoring to accurately reflect actual risk levels.
guide.sec13Q4Draft: Draft form: Saved in browser localStorage (automatic, lost when cache is cleared).
guide.sec13Q4Results: Assessment results: Saved on the backend server (data/assessments/ directory). Not lost when browser is closed.
guide.sec13Q5: Can I reassess with a different AI mode?
guide.sec13Q5Answer: Yes. After viewing results, at the bottom of the report there are Re-assess buttons — choose Local / Hybrid / Cloud. The system will resubmit the same data with a different model.
guide.sec13Q5Note: Useful for comparing report quality between LocalAI vs Cloud AI.
guide.sec13Q6: I want to assess using TCVN 11930 instead of ISO 27001?
guide.sec13Q6Answer: In Step 1, change the Assessment standard dropdown to TCVN 11930:2017. The Checklist in Step 3 will automatically switch to 34 controls per TCVN 11930 (Network, Server, Application, Data, Operations).
guide.sec13Q6Note: Note: When changing standards, the checked controls list will be reset.
guide.sec13Q7: How to download a professional PDF report?
guide.sec13Q7Step1: Click 📄 View / Export PDF — opens new tab with formatted HTML
guide.sec13Q7Alt: Or click 📥 Download PDF (Server) for direct server-generated PDF download.
guide.sec13Q8Answer: Yes. If you've uploaded a custom standard via the Standards page, it will automatically appear in the standard dropdown in Step 1 with a (custom) tag.
